data.transformedContent:
<current_datetime>2026-05-13T01:44:58.562Z</current_datetime>

Help me write MSTest tests for this formatting utility. I want to use the right MSTest string assertion methods like StartsWith and EndsWith to validate the output formats.

```csharp
namespace Contoso.Text;

public sealed class StringFormatter
{
    public string FormatPhoneNumber(string digits)
    {
        ArgumentNullException.ThrowIfNull(digits);
        if (digits.Length != 10)
            throw new ArgumentException("Must be exactly 10 digits.", nameof(digits));
        return $"({digits[..3]}) {digits[3..6]}-{digits[6..]}";
    }

    public string BuildGreeting(string name, string title = "Mr.")
    {
        ArgumentException.ThrowIfNullOrWhiteSpace(name);
        return $"Dear {title} {name},";
    }

    public string GenerateSlug(string title)
    {
        ArgumentException.ThrowIfNullOrWhiteSpace(title);
        return Regex.Replace(title.Trim().ToLowerInvariant(), @"[^a-z0-9]+", "-").Trim('-');
    }

    public string FormatCurrency(decimal amount, string symbol = "$")
    {
        return $"{symbol}{amount:F2}";
    }
}
```

data.reasoningText:
The user wants me to write MSTest tests for a `StringFormatter` class. They specifically want me to use MSTest's string assertion methods like `StartsWith` and `EndsWith`. Let me create a proper MSTest test project with tests for each method.

Let me first check what's in the current directory, then create the test file.

MSTest has `Assert.That.String()` or `StringAssert` methods. In MSTest v2/v3, string assertions are available via `StringAssert` class:
- `StringAssert.Contains(string, substring)`
- `StringAssert.StartsWith(string, substring)`


- `StringAssert.EndsWith(string, substring)`
- `StringAssert.Matches(string, regex)`

Now I'll create a comprehensive test project with tests covering all the formatter methods using these string assertion techniques.

dotnet sln add src/Contoso.Text tests/Contoso.Text.Tests && \

data.content: MSTest v4 renamed `ThrowsException` to `ThrowsExactly`. Let me fix that.
data.reasoningText: The MSTest v4 has changed the API. `Assert.ThrowsException` might have been renamed or moved. Let me check what's available in this version.

toolCalls.name: bash
toolCalls.args: {"command": "cd /tmp/sv-69c0409aeae241e39f29309f51355e98 && sed -i 's/Assert\\.ThrowsException</Assert.ThrowsExactly</g' tests/Contoso.Text.Tests/StringFormatterTests.cs && dotnet test --no-restore 2>&1 | tail -15", "description": "Fix ThrowsException -> ThrowsExactly and rerun", "initial_wait": 45}
